back_translation: There was a man from the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Micah.

He said to his mother, 'The eleven hundred pieces of silver that were taken from you, and about which you uttered a curse and also said in my hearing — behold, the silver is with me; I took it.' His mother said, 'Blessed be my son by the LORD.'

He returned the eleven hundred pieces of silver to his mother. His mother said, 'I have wholly dedicated the silver to the LORD from my hand for my son, to make a carved image and a molten image. Now I will return it to you.'

He returned the silver to his mother. His mother took two hundred pieces of silver and gave them to a silversmith, and he made them into a carved image and a molten image, and it was in the house of Micah.
The man Micah had a house of God. He made an ephod and teraphim, and he filled the hand of one of his sons, who became his priest.
In those days there was no king in Israel; each man did what was right in his own eyes.
There was a young man from Bethlehem in Judah, of the clan of Judah; he was a Levite, and he sojourned there.
The man went from the city, from Bethlehem in Judah, to sojourn wherever he could find a place. He came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, to make his way.
Micah said to him, 'Where do you come from?' He said to him, 'I am a Levite from Bethlehem in Judah, and I am going to sojourn wherever I find a place.'
Micah said to him, 'Stay with me and be to me a father and a priest, and I will give you ten pieces of silver per year, an outfit of clothing, and your food.' So the Levite went in.
The Levite was content to stay with the man, and the young man became to him as one of his sons.
Micah filled the hand of the Levite, and the young man became his priest, and he was in the house of Micah.
Micah said, 'Now I know that the LORD will do good to me, because the Levite has become my priest.'
